title: ttok4bedrock
tagline: Token counting for Amazon Bedrock — a ttok-compatible drop-in replacement

chapters.title: Interface Layer
chapters.description: How users interact with ttok4bedrock — through the Python SDK or the command-line interface. Both are thin wrappers that delegate all real work to the core engine.
chapters.diagram:
graph LR user([User]) --> sdk["SDK
count_tokens / truncate"] user --> cli["CLI
ttok4bedrock command"] sdk --> engine["BedrockTokenCounter"] cli --> engine engine --> api["Bedrock CountTokens API"]

chapters.title: Core Engine
chapters.description: The BedrockTokenCounter class — LRU caching, message overhead removal, API integration, and an adaptive truncation algorithm that converges in 3-5 API calls.
chapters.diagram:
graph TD
  input["Input Text"] --> cache{"LRU Cache"}
  cache -->|hit| result["Token Count"]
  cache -->|miss| format["Format as Bedrock Message"]
  format --> api["CountTokens API"]
  api --> overhead["Subtract Overhead"]
  overhead --> result

knowledge_checks.question: Why does ttok4bedrock subtract a 'message overhead' from the raw API token count?
options: Because the Bedrock API wraps text in a message structure that adds extra tokens
To round down to the nearest integer
To compensate for model-specific tokenizer differences
knowledge_checks.explanation: The CountTokens API counts tokens for the entire message payload including the JSON structure (role, content fields). Subtracting the overhead gives users the intuitive count for just their text content.

knowledge_checks.question: What is the main benefit of the LRU cache in the truncation algorithm?
options: It avoids repeated API calls for the same text, which is critical when the truncation loop tests overlapping substrings
knowledge_checks.explanation: The adaptive truncation algorithm may test many substrings that share prefixes. The LRU cache ensures that if the same substring is counted twice, the second call is instant instead of a ~200ms API round-trip.

labs.title: Public SDK
labs.tagline: The two-function API that makes ttok4bedrock a drop-in replacement for ttok
labs.description: This module is the public face of the library. It exposes just two functions — count_tokens() and truncate() — that mirror the original ttok interface. Each function creates a fresh BedrockTokenCounter and delegates immediately, keeping the API surface minimal.
learning_objectives: Understand the facade pattern: a simple public API hiding complex internals
See how lazy imports keep module loading fast
Recognize the design decision to create a new counter per call vs. reusing one

exercises.prompt: The SDK creates a new BedrockTokenCounter on every call, which means the LRU cache is never reused across calls. Modify count_tokens() to use a module-level counter instance and observe the cache hit rate difference.
exercises.hint: Create a _default_counter at module level (or use a factory with a cache). Check counter.get_cache_info() before and after repeated calls.

labs.title: CLI Entry Point
labs.tagline: A Click-based command that provides ttok-compatible token counting from the terminal
labs.description: The CLI accepts text from arguments, files, or stdin — just like the original ttok. It adds Bedrock-specific options (--aws-region, -m for model ID) and translates AWS exceptions into user-friendly error messages.
learning_objectives: Understand how Click decorators compose a CLI interface declaratively
See the input priority logic: arguments > file > stdin
Learn the error handling strategy: catch specific AWS exceptions and translate them

exercises.prompt: Add a --verbose flag that prints the model ID and region being used before counting. How does Click's decorator pattern make this easy to add?
exercises.hint: Add @click.option('--verbose', is_flag=True) and use it in the function body before the counter call.

labs.title: Core Engine
labs.tagline: BedrockTokenCounter — caching, overhead removal, API integration, and adaptive truncation
labs.description: This is where the real work happens. The BedrockTokenCounter class wraps the Bedrock CountTokens API with three key innovations: (1) LRU caching to minimize API calls, (2) automatic message overhead subtraction for intuitive token counts, and (3) an adaptive truncation algorithm that converges in 3-5 API calls by learning the character-to-token ratio from each measurement.
learning_objectives: Understand how functools.lru_cache is applied to an instance method via a wrapper pattern
Learn why message overhead subtraction is necessary and how it's computed
Trace the adaptive truncation algorithm: initial estimation → ratio refinement → fine-tuning
See how text characteristics (punctuation, word length) improve the initial estimate

exercises.prompt: The truncation algorithm uses a self-imposed limit of 20 API calls. Try truncating a very long text (10,000+ characters) to 5 tokens with return_metadata=True. How many API calls does it actually use? What happens if you lower the limit to 5?
exercises.hint: Use counter.truncate(long_text, 5, model_id, return_metadata=True) and inspect the api_calls field in the metadata dict.

exercises.prompt: The smart estimation adjusts chars_per_token based on punctuation density, space ratio, and average word length. Create two test strings — one with many short words and punctuation, one with long technical terms — and compare how many API calls the truncation needs for each.
exercises.hint: Try 'Hi! Yes. No. OK? Wow!' vs 'electroencephalography neuropsychopharmacology' and use return_metadata=True.
